SnowDistribution: Good snow cover above 800 metres on most aspects.  Cold clear and sunny conditions.  Light winds approx 10mph or less.
Comment: Cloudy on Thursday with rain and snow. Paths and water courses will be icy.
WeatherInfluences: It will be dry throughout the period with the freezing level around 200 metres, rising slightly during the afternoon. Moderate East-South-Easterly winds strengthen through Tuesday night becoming fresh to strong through the day.
SnowStability: Initially little change overnight with existing instabilities present in steep locations on mainly North-East to South-East aspects above 800 metres. For the remainder of the period this existing windslab will begin to consolidate then redistribution of snow forming new unstable windslab mainly on SW to NW aspects above 800m. These are not expected to be as extensive but will be poorly bonded. Cornices will begin to form above these new aspects.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A bright, clear and sunny day with light North-Easterly winds. The freezing level lifted slightly during the morning to around 300 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: There was a light covering of new snow overnight including to lower elevations mostly in wind sheltered locations. Above 800 metres unstable windslab is present mainly in steep locations on North-East to South-East aspects. Other aspects and elevations are affected, these areas are less extensive. Fragile cornices are present above these locations with debris observed in the coire from yesterday. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Good visibility. Winds approx 10 - 20 mph.  Fresh snow at all altitudes - hard going on foot in places.
Comment: High pressure will be replaced on Thursday by a Westerly airflow bringing more unsettled conditions.
WeatherInfluences: A dry period with the freezing level around 250 metres overnight, rising to 450 metres during the day. Fresh, South-Easterly winds will increase through Tuesday night to reach gale force by dawn.
SnowStability: Fresh windslab accumulations will develop in wind-sheltered locations due to snow redistribution. Existing weaknesses will also persist. Areas most affected will be West through North to East aspects above 850 metres. Fragile cornices will develop above Northerly aspects. Generally good stability elsewhere with wind exposed locations being firm and icy. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Overnight snowfall and very strong Northerly winds eased to leave a dry and bright day with the freezing level around 350 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Weaknesses are persisting within windslab accumulations on steep wind-sheltered terrain above 850 metres. Aspects most affected are North-West through North to Southerly aspects. Only small fresh cornices were observed. Elsewhere there is generally good stability with wind exposed areas being firm. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Snow cover above 400 metres with some deeper accumulations and drifted snow above 800 metres. North-Westerly winds approx 15 mph at summit level calmer after midday with great visibility at all levels.
Comment: Unsettled Westerly flow approaching and becoming milder, spells of rain and summit snow through Thursday and Friday, with some heavy outbreaks especially on Friday.  Winds becoming Southerly and remaining strong with a risk of gales.
WeatherInfluences: Overnight, cold conditions will give a frost at all levels with the freezing around 200 metres rising to 700 metres by the end of the day. Mainly dry with South-Easterly winds becoming strong Tuesday night with gales developing through the day.
SnowStability: Snowpack instabilities will continue to slowly  consolidate in the strengthening variable winds.  However, some pockets of moderately bonded windslab will still persist in some steep wind sheltered locations and wind redistribution forming new shallow deposits of poorly bonded windslab. Most affected locations will be sheltered gullies, steep slopes and coire rims with a W through N to E aspects above 850m. Fragile cornices may exist above similar aspects. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: After a cold night and a dusting of overnight snow it dawned bright and clear with stunning views, a prefect winters day. The freezing level was around 200 metres, winds were moderate North-Westerly at first then eased and veered to the North-East and it was dry and eventually quite calm .
ObservedSnowStability: Some of the snowpack instabilities have partially consolidated however, some pockets of poorly bonded windslab still persist in steep wind sheltered locations.  Areas mostly affected are sheltered gullies, steep slopes and coire rims with a  North-West through East to South-Easterly aspects above 850 metres. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Good snow cover on all aspects. Good visibility. Winds around 20 mph dropping in the afternoon.
Comment: After a cold day on Wednesday temperatures will rise over the next few days with snow, rain and strong South to South-Westerly winds.
WeatherInfluences: South-East winds increase overnight to become gale force during the day. It will remain dry and cold.
SnowStability: Areas of unstable windslab will persist mainly on North through East to South-West aspects above 900 metres. During the period further unstable windslab will develop on West to North aspects above 700 metres due to wind redistribution. The older snowpack will remain firm and stable. Recent cornices will be fragile. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a cold, dry, sunny day with moderate North-Easterly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Poorly bonded windslab is present on many aspects with North to East aspects above 900 metres most affected. Recent cornices are fragile. The older snowpack is firm and stable. One human triggered avalanche recorded on an East aspect at 1200 metres. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Firm snow - ice on most aspects Good visibility.   Winds approx 40 - 50 mph making progress very difficult and strenuous.
Comment: The majority of the snowpack is firm and icy. Localised windslab will be encountered only in sheltered terrain.
WeatherInfluences: Remaining cold with the freezing level sub 500 metres throughout. A cloudy day with only a few snow showers expected. Winds, gale to strong gale force throughout the day from the South-South-East.
SnowStability: The strong winds will redistribute existing snow deposits throughout the day. New moderately to weakly bonded windslab will develop on West through North to East aspects above 900 metres. Gully tops, scarp slopes and corrie rims will be worst affected. Instabilities will also be encountered at lower levels in steep sheltered terrain. The majority of the snowpack will be very firm and icy. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Overnight, there was snowfall to the glens but the day was dry and sunny. Cold throughout, around -7 with a high wind chill. Winds were storm force overnight but easing during the day.
ObservedSnowStability: Moderately to weakly bonded windslab is present in wind sheltered locations on North-East through South-East to South-West aspects above 900 metres Corrie rims, gully tops and steep convex slopes are most affected.  Moderately bonded windslab is present below this altitude but the majority of the snowpack is firm and icy.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Hard snow ice on exposed windward slopes.  Excellent visibility. Winds 20 - 30 mph easing through the morning.
Comment: Gale force winds developing through the day presenting challenging conditions.
WeatherInfluences: South-South-East winds will increase through the evening, reaching gale force by midday. The freezing level will be between 300 and 400 metres for the majority of the period. No precipitation is forecasted.
SnowStability: Moderately to weakly bonded windslab will start to develop on West through North to North-East aspects above 900 metres due to the redistribution of existing snow. Instabilities will persist within deep deposits on East to South aspects. Gully tops, coire rims, crag aprons and steep convexities most affected with localised instabilities affecting lower elevations such as river beds and steep banks. Wind exposed areas will be stable, firm and icy. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Overnight snow to low levels cleared to leave a dry and sunny day. Strong winds from the North-West, eased through the day becoming light. Midday summit temperature of minus 9 degrees Celsius.
ObservedSnowStability: Poorly bonded windslab has developed on East to South aspects above 900 metres. Moderately bonded windslab persists on North-West to North-East aspects. Steep wind sheltered areas are most affected such as convexities, gully tops, coire rims and crag aprons. Cornices are also found above these same aspects. Wind exposed areas are stable, firm and icy. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
